questions.title: Enrollment Duplicate Check Mismatch Between bookTrial and submitEnrollment
questions.description: bookTrial checks for duplicates using parentEmail + childName + programId (per child per program), while submitEnrollment checks using only parentEmail + programId (per parent per program). GP-01 policy states 'one enrollment per child per program'. These two endpoints are inconsistent in enforcing the same business rule.
questions.impact: A parent with two children can book trials for both in the same program, but only one can enroll. This creates a confusing user experience and violates GP-01.

options.label: Align both to per-child-per-program (parentEmail + childName + programId)
options.resolutionEffect: Change submitEnrollment duplicate check to match bookTrial: parentEmail + childName + programId. Both endpoints now enforce GP-01 consistently. A parent can enroll multiple children in the same program. childName comparison uses case-insensitive trimmed matching.
cons: Edge case: parent enters 'John' vs 'Johnny' — treated as different children

options.label: Align both to per-parent-per-program (parentEmail + programId)
options.resolutionEffect: Change bookTrial duplicate check to match submitEnrollment: parentEmail + programId. Only one trial booking and one enrollment per parent per program. Multi-child families must use separate email addresses.
pros: Prevents any form of double submission

options.label: Add childName + childAge composite key to enrollment uniqueness
options.resolutionEffect: Add a database-level unique constraint @@unique([parentEmail, childName, programId]) on both Trial and Enrollment models. Enforces GP-01 at the DB level in addition to application-level checks.
cons: childName normalization must happen before DB insert
DB constraint error messages are less user-friendly
Overly rigid for names that might vary slightly

questions.selectionRationale: Application-level check with normalized childName (trim + lowercase) is the right balance. DB-level unique constraint (CON-Q01-C) is too rigid for name variations. The OpenAPI source (bookTrial) has the correct behavior — submitEnrollment should match it.

conflictSources.fe_details: submitEnrollment UI may not emphasize per-child distinction
conflictSources.prisma_contract: No unique constraint defined for this combination on Enrollment model

questions.title: Contract Status Mismatch on admin_dashboard Module
questions.description: The admin_dashboard module has a status discrepancy between its source artifacts. The OpenAPI spec has x-contract-status: 'draft' while the FE details has status: 'agreed'. This means the API contract may not be considered finalized by the backend team while the frontend considers it done.
questions.impact: Low — the admin_dashboard has 4 simple GET endpoints with no write operations. The discrepancy is likely an oversight since all endpoints, schemas, and behaviors are fully defined in both sources.

options.label: Align to 'agreed' (FE is correct)
options.resolutionEffect: Treat the admin_dashboard contract as agreed/finalized. The OpenAPI 'draft' status is an oversight — all 4 endpoints are fully specified with response schemas, error handling, and security. Update OpenAPI x-contract-status to 'agreed'.
pros: All specs are complete — no actual draft content

options.label: Keep as 'draft' until explicitly reviewed
options.resolutionEffect: Treat admin_dashboard as draft. Flag for explicit review before implementation. May delay dashboard implementation.
cons: No actual gaps or issues to resolve in review

questions.selectionRationale: The admin_dashboard contract is fully specified with no open questions. The 'draft' label is an oversight — all 4 endpoints have complete schemas, security, and error handling. Aligning to 'agreed' prevents unnecessary delays.

questions.title: RBAC Source Gap — Admin Role Inferred from FE Across 8 Modules
questions.description: All 42 protected endpoints derive their 'admin' role from FE allowedRoles, not from OpenAPI x-required-roles annotations. The OpenAPI specs specify cookieAuth (authentication) but not which roles are required (authorization). This is a systematic gap affecting auth_accounts, admin_dashboard, settings, lead_management, calendar_scheduling, program_pricing, payment_tracking, enrollment_trial, and communication modules.
questions.impact: Low for MVP (single-role app with only 'admin'). Becomes critical if additional roles are added in Phase 2 (e.g., staff, instructor). The OpenAPI spec is technically incomplete for authorization.

options.label: Accept FE roles as authoritative for MVP, document for Phase 2
options.resolutionEffect: Use FE allowedRoles as the RBAC source. Implement role_permission_check middleware with admin role on all 42 protected endpoints. Document that OpenAPI x-required-roles should be added before introducing additional roles in Phase 2.
pros: FE and BE agree on the role (admin)
cons: OpenAPI spec remains technically incomplete for authorization

options.label: Add x-required-roles to all OpenAPI specs now
options.resolutionEffect: Update all 9 OpenAPI specs to add x-required-roles: ['admin'] on every protected endpoint. Makes the API contract self-contained for authorization.

options.label: Define roles in global middleware registry only
options.resolutionEffect: Define the role-to-endpoint mapping in global_middleware_registry.json (already partially exists). Endpoints reference the registry rather than carrying role annotations. Centralizes RBAC config.
pros: Single source of truth for RBAC
Easy to update when adding roles

questions.selectionRationale: For a single-role MVP, accepting FE roles is pragmatic. The role inference is consistent across all modules and the global_security_policies.json already documents this pattern. Adding x-required-roles to OpenAPI is a Phase 2 prerequisite, not an MVP blocker.

conflictSources.openapi: security: ['cookieAuth'] — authentication only, no role annotation
conflictSources.fe_details: allowedRoles: ['admin'] — explicit role requirement on every protected screen
conflictSources.prisma_contract: N/A — RBAC not defined at DB level
